# Vendor spec status — read this before trusting these files

GLS confirmed (2026-05-26, reaffirmed 2026-06 by Timo) that the OpenAPI
YAML files distributed via their Developer Portal are **not actively
maintained** and must **not** be used to build the ShipIT integration.
The shipment-processing OpenAPI (`shipit-farm.yaml`) has been **removed**
from this folder for that reason.

## Authoritative source: the doxygen REST docs (mirrored here)

The single source of truth for shipment processing, tracking, parcel
shop and sporadic collection is the doxygen-generated REST API
documentation, mirrored offline under [`doxygen/`](./doxygen/):

  https://shipit.gls-group.eu/webservices/5_0_11/doxygen/WS-REST-API/index.html

| Page | Covers |
|------|--------|
| `doxygen/rest_shipment_processing.html` | createParcels request/response, every Service, products, printing options, custom content |
| `doxygen/rest_web_service_requests_samples.html` | Full verbatim request/response samples per scenario |
| `doxygen/rest_webservice_fault_responses_samples.html` | Error/fault response shapes |
| `doxygen/rest_tracking.html` | Track & Trace |
| `doxygen/rest_parcel_shop.html` | Parcel shop lookup |
| `doxygen/rest_sporadic_collection.html` | Sporadic collection (pickup) |

The connector's `schemas/*.json` sample payloads — from which
`./generate` infers the typed modules in `karrio/schemas/gls/` — are
built to match these doxygen pages, **not** the YAML.

### GLS-validated request samples

[`gls-validated-samples/`](./gls-validated-samples/) holds the concrete
`createParcels` request bodies Timo (GLS) shared as the reference for a
correct request (addressee-only, signature, flex-delivery, pick&return,
shop-return). They are the ground truth the connector output is matched
against — see that folder's `README.md`.

### Wire facts that bit us (all confirmed against the doxygen)

- **`Shipment.Middleware` is mandatory** and carries the fixed value
  `JTLviaGLS` — this is how GLS attributes billing to JTL. It is **not**
  a per-connection partner reference (an earlier revision wrongly fed a
  configurable `partner_reference` here).
- **There is no top-level `PartnerReference` field.** It was invented by
  an earlier revision and GLS rejects requests that include unknown
  fields — removed.
- **`Shipment.Identifier` is optional** ("person who started the parcel
  creation"); we send it only when an `app_identifier` is configured.
- **Service names are the fixed `service_*` codes** (e.g.
  `service_saturday`, `service_flexdelivery`, `service_shopdelivery`,
  `service_pickandreturn`, `service_addonliability`,
  `service_limitedquantities`). The PascalCase names an earlier revision
  sent (`SaturdayService`, …) are the response-side `ServiceArea.Header`
  labels, not request `ServiceName` values.
- **There is no `Volume` section in ShipIT.** Parcel dimensions are not
  carried on the create-shipment wire; the API is weight-based. (Any
  volumetric/scanner-station block was removed.)
- `Product` is just `PARCEL` or `EXPRESS`; the actual delivery network
  (BusinessParcel / EuroBusinessParcel / GlobalBusinessParcel / …) is
  derived by GLS from the address + weight.
- `IncotermCode` is a **2-digit numeric code** (`10` = DDP, `20` = DAP, …),
  required when shipping between commercial zones.
- `AlternativeShipperAddress` is cosmetic (label cover address only); the
  operational shipper is always the registered Contact-ID. Multiple
  warehouses need multiple Contact-IDs.

## YAMLs still kept here (separate APIs, not covered by the doxygen)

The doxygen does **not** document these GLS APIs, so their portal specs
remain the only reference and stay committed:

- `customs-consignments-v3.yaml` — Customs API v3.3.0. The types in
  `karrio/schemas/gls/customs_consignment_request.py` are generated by
  `./generate` from `schemas/customs_consignment_request.json`, a sample
  payload built to exercise every field. When the YAML is refreshed,
  update the sample JSON and rerun `./generate`.
- `document-management-v1.yaml` — Customs Document Management (paperless
  trade upload).
- `ecitrackandtrace.yaml` — Track & Trace `ParcelDTO.status` enum backing
  `units.TrackingStatus`.
- `authentification-service-v2.yaml` — OAuth2 token service.

Treat these as last-maintained references: cross-check live behaviour
before trusting an individual field.
